title: Split delivery judges + add hooks-based judge
description: Move TypesafeJudge into judge_typesafe.go, add HooksJudge (judge_hooks.go) reading ~/data/arcmux/sessions/<id>.json mutated by an 'arcmux hook' CLI from claude+codex hooks. Config [delivery].judge picks one judge. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-judge-split-and-hooks-judge-design.md

title: Session state never transitions working->idle (tmux transport): stuck 'working' forever after agent finishes
description: After a prompt is delivered SetState(working) is called, but nothing ever transitions a tmux-backed session back to idle: the health monitor only does idle->working, working->stuck, and ->exited; the hook watcher tails Stop events into a map that no consumer reads. Observed: codex and claude both report state=working 70s+ after answering and going visually idle at their prompt. Fix: add a screen-quiescence working->idle transition to the health monitor (output unchanged for a quiescence window AND no WorkingIndicator present).
close_reason: Fixed: added screen-quiescence working->idle transition to health monitor (quiescentIdle + idleQuiescence, per-session runState). Guarded by WorkingIndicator so a busy-but-quiet agent is not falsely idled; added WorkingIndicator 'esc to interrupt' to claude profile (codex already had 'Working'). Verified live on both agents incl. a 25s silent task that correctly stayed 'working'. Unit tests added in health/monitor_test.go.

title: Claude handshake marks session 'failed': ready pattern '>' never matches v2.x prompt glyph '❯'
description: The claude profile ReadyPattern is '>' but Claude Code v2.x renders its prompt as '❯' (U+276F) and shows no bare '>' on screen, so waitForReadyPattern times out after 25s and performHandshake fails -> session state set to 'failed'. The pane is actually fully ready (Remote Control active) and SendPrompt works, so the 'failed' state is a false negative that misleads status/ready callers. Fix: use a ReadyPattern that matches a genuinely-ready Claude TUI (e.g. the persistent footer 'bypass permissions on'), not the pre-launch shell prompt glyph.
close_reason: Fixed: claude profile ReadyPattern '>' -> 'Remote Control active' (matches the --remote-control footer present in both default and HTTP launch paths; '>' never matched v2.x glyph '❯'). Verified live: handshake now reaches idle, not failed. Regression test added in profile_test.go.

# Replace per-session Claude hook scripts with one session-aware hook

## Problem
Boyan directive from Discord 1509663488170131686: arcmux currently writes ~/.claude/hooks/arcmux-s-<session>.sh per Claude session. Change it so arcmux installs/uses a single generic hook script that derives the session-specific output path from environment supplied to the Claude session (for example ARCMUX_SESSION_ID and ARCMUX_HOOK_OUTPUT_DIR). Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior.

Follow-up design directive from Discord 1509665898401890495: if a shared handoff point is needed, use a /tmp/arcmux directory where the tmux session creator writes the session-specific environment, and make the tmux loader read that environment before launching claude/codex/etc. This keeps the hook generic while making session identity/runtime paths available to the launched agent process.

## Acceptance
No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; if file-backed env handoff is needed, the session creator writes a per-session env file under /tmp/arcmux and the tmux loader loads it before launching the agent; /tmp/arcmux and env files are permissioned safely and parsed/escaped so an untrusted writable file cannot inject shell; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, tmux-loader env loading, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.

Post-merge deployment acceptance from Discord 1509668087086059530: after the refactor merges, rebuild/restart the live `test-elonco` elonco/arcmux runtime so this profile is actually running the new generic-hook + /tmp/arcmux env-handoff behavior. Verify on the live restarted profile that no new per-session hook scripts are created, the generic hook/env path is active, and existing stale `arcmux-s-*.sh` cleanup has run or is demonstrably ignored/removed.

## Duplicate Search

Remove/clean up existing generated session-specific hooks like arcmux-s-*.sh. Preserve arcmux's watcher/status/ready behavior. No new per-session Claude hook script files are created; one generic arcmux hook is installed/updated idempotently; spawned Claude sessions receive the env needed for that hook to write the correct per-session JSONL; existing arcmux-s-*.sh files are removed/ignored by migration/cleanup; tests cover install idempotency, env-derived output path, cleanup, and watcher/status compatibility; PR/merge documents operational cleanup.
